title: The Teacher Who Changed Everything
description: Relative clauses (who / which / that / whose / where). Defining vs non-defining — when commas matter, and why the missing comma can change a life.
summary_pt_br: Orações relativas: WHO (pessoas), WHICH (coisas), THAT (ambos, só em restritivas), WHOSE (posse), WHERE (lugar). Restritivas SEM vírgula (essenciais); não-restritivas COM vírgula (extras). A diferença muda o sentido: 'My brother who lives in NY' x 'My brother, who lives in NY' — a segunda sugere que só tenho UM irmão.

scenes.text_en:
Zara wrote two nearly identical sentences:
   'My brother WHO lives in London is a chef.'
   'My brother, WHO LIVES IN LONDON, is a chef.'
'Same words. Different meaning,' she said.
'Without commas: I have MORE than one brother — this is the London one.
With commas: I have ONE brother, and by the way, he lives in London.'
scenes.text_pt:
A Zara escreveu duas frases quase iguais:
   'My brother WHO lives in London is a chef.'
   'My brother, WHO LIVES IN LONDON, is a chef.'
'Mesmas palavras. Sentidos diferentes,' ela disse.
'Sem vírgula: tenho MAIS de um irmão — esse é o de Londres.
Com vírgula: tenho UM irmão e, aliás, ele mora em Londres.'

scenes.title: Defining vs non-defining — the comma rule
scenes.body_en: DEFINING (no commas) = the clause NARROWS which noun. Essential info. 'The teacher WHO teaches B1 is Zara.' Can use THAT instead of who/which. NON-DEFINING (with commas) = adds extra info, not essential. 'Zara, WHO teaches B1, is from Manchester.' Cannot use THAT here — only who/which/whose.
scenes.body_pt: RESTRITIVA (sem vírgula) = restringe o substantivo; info essencial. Pode trocar who/which por THAT. NÃO-RESTRITIVA (entre vírgulas) = info extra; NÃO pode usar THAT, só who/which/whose. A vírgula literalmente muda o sentido.

examples.en: The surfer who won the contest is Bia. (defining)
Bia, who is 16, won the contest. (non-defining)

items.example_en: the student whose bag is red

exercise.question: 'Rafa, ___ truck is yellow, sells tacos.' Which relative pronoun?
exercise.explanation: WHOSE = possession. The truck belongs to Rafa — so 'Rafa, whose truck is yellow'.
exercise.hint_pt_br: WHOSE = posse. 'O caminhão DELE é amarelo' → Rafa, whose truck…

exercise.question: Complete: 'This is the café ___ Mr. Kumar works.' (lugar)
exercise.explanation: WHERE for places. 'The café where he works'.

exercise.question: Which sentence is NON-DEFINING (extra info)?
options: The book that I lent you was expensive
The student who speaks five languages is Maya
My mother, who lives in Rio, called yesterday
The song which we heard was by Ed Sheeran
exercise.explanation: Option C has commas = non-defining = extra info. (The implication: she has only one mother.)
exercise.hint_pt_br: Não-restritiva tem vírgulas e dá info extra. Geralmente implica 'só tenho um X'.

scenes.location_en: Zara's office, editing a student's essay
scenes.location_pt: Sala da Zara, editando a redação de um aluno

turns.en: Can I say 'the dog that bit Cosmo' or 'the dog which bit Cosmo'?
turns.pt: Posso dizer 'the dog that bit Cosmo' ou 'the dog which bit Cosmo'?

turns.en: Both in defining. THAT is a bit more natural in speech.
turns.pt: Os dois em restritivas. THAT é mais natural na fala.

turns.en: No — with a comma, use WHICH. Never THAT in non-defining.
turns.pt: Não — com vírgula, use WHICH. Nunca THAT em não-restritiva.

exercise.explanation: Five pronouns, five jobs. Remember: THAT doesn't survive the comma.
exercise.hint_pt_br: THAT não resiste à vírgula. Em não-restritiva use only who/which/whose.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "The Teacher Who Changed Everything". Relative clauses (who / which / that / whose / where). Defining vs non-defining — when commas matter, and why the missing comma can change a life. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "The Teacher Who Changed Everything". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'The Teacher Who Changed Everything'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "The Teacher Who Changed Everything".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.
